drugs.notes: 825mg/m² twice a day on the days of radiotherapy only (28 days in total: days 1-5, 8-12, 15-19, 22-26, 29-33, 36-38). Dose banded per national dose bands. In patients aged 71+ or significant intercurrent illness, dose may be reduced to 500mg/m². First dose administered in the evening of day 1.

Intravenous bolus in water for injection over 10 minutes. Maximum single dose 20mg. Maximum cumulative dose 28mg/m² or 56mg. Dose rounded to nearest 1mg (up if halfway). Vesicant - extravasation precautions required. In patients aged 71+ or significant intercurrent illness, dose reduced to 10mg/m² (maximum 20mg).

pre_medications: Dexamethasone 8mg oral or intravenous (day 1, 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy)
Metoclopramide 10mg oral or intravenous (day 1, 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy)
Dexamethasone 4mg oral once a day for 3 days (take home, starting day after mitomycin)
Metoclopramide 10mg oral three times a day when required (take home)
Ciprofloxacin 250mg oral twice a day for 42 days
Loperamide 2mg oral every two hours once first liquid stool appears (max 48 hours, max 16mg/day)
special_warnings: Concurrent radiotherapy: Capecitabine given on days of radiotherapy ONLY (total 28 days). Radiotherapy normally scheduled 5 consecutive days per 7-day cycle.
DPD testing required: All patients must be tested for DPD deficiency before initiation (cycle 1). Patients with complete or partial DPD deficiency are at increased risk of severe and fatal toxicity.
Mitomycin is a vesicant - extravasation precautions required.
Maximum cumulative dose of mitomycin is 28mg/m² or 56mg.
If chest pain occurs consider stopping capecitabine.
Total daily dose of capecitabine must be divided into two doses 12 hours apart. First dose administered in evening of day 1. Serious toxicity has occurred where total daily dose given twice a day.
This is a short course of oral chemotherapy that must not be continued beyond the prescribed schedule.
NPSA/2008/RRR001 must be followed when prescribing, dispensing or administering oral chemotherapy.
FBC, LFTs and U&Es required prior to day 1 and then every 7 days during treatment.
Neutrophil must be ≥1.5x10⁹/L and platelets ≥100x10⁹/L prior to prescribing.
In those aged 71+ and/or significant intercurrent illness: capecitabine may be reduced to 500mg/m², mitomycin reduced to 10mg/m² (max 20mg).

drugs.notes: 1250mg/m² twice a day, days 1-14 inclusive and days 22-35 inclusive. Dose banded per national dose bands. Start on evening of day 1 and day 22. Take with or after food.
Intravenous bolus over 10 minutes. Maximum dose 14mg. Dose rounded to nearest 1mg (up if halfway). Vesicant - extravasation precautions required.

dose_modifications.notes: Delay mitomycin until levels achieved; re-start at 75% for 14-day delay. Little need to reduce capecitabine for haematological toxicity.

pre_medications: Dexamethasone 8mg oral or intravenous once only (day 1, 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy)
Metoclopramide 10mg oral or intravenous once only (day 1, 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy)
Dexamethasone 4mg oral once a day for 3 days (take home, starting day after mitomycin)
Metoclopramide 10mg oral three times a day when required (take home)
Loperamide 4mg oral after first loose stool then 2-4mg four times a day when required (max 16mg/24 hours)
special_warnings: DPD testing required: All patients must be tested for DPD deficiency before initiation (cycle 1). Patients with complete or partial DPD deficiency are at increased risk of severe and fatal toxicity.
Mitomycin is a vesicant - extravasation precautions required.
Maximum dose of mitomycin is 14mg.
If chest pain occurs consider stopping capecitabine.

Total daily dose of capecitabine must be divided into two doses 12 hours apart. First dose administered in evening of day 1 (and day 22). Serious toxicity has occurred where total daily dose given twice a day.
Capecitabine should be taken with or after food.
Different variations of this regimen are used in conjunction with radiotherapy for treatment of anal cancer. Always check the indication and apply the correct protocol.
This is a short course of oral chemotherapy that must not be continued beyond the prescribed schedule.
NPSA/2008/RRR001 must be followed when prescribing, dispensing or administering oral chemotherapy.
Patients with metastatic disease assessed for response after 12 weeks; if stable or responded, further 12 weeks may be given.
Neutrophil must be ≥1.5x10⁹/L and platelets ≥100x10⁹/L prior to prescribing.

drugs.notes: Intravenous infusion in 500ml glucose 5% over 120 minutes. Dose banded per national dose bands (5mg/ml). Exfoliant - extravasation precautions required. Rare: acute interstitial lung disease/lung fibrosis - stop if unexplained respiratory symptoms.
1000mg/m² twice a day, days 1-14. Dose banded per national dose bands. Start on evening of day 1. Take with or after food. For patients aged 70+, consider starting at 800mg/m² twice a day.

special_warnings: DPD testing required: All patients must be tested for DPD deficiency before initiation (cycle 1). Patients with complete or partial DPD deficiency are at increased risk of severe and fatal toxicity.

Capecitabine should be taken with or after food.

NPSA/2008/RRR001 must be followed when prescribing, dispensing or administering oral chemotherapy.
Neutrophil must be ≥1.5x10⁹/L and platelets ≥100x10⁹/L prior to prescribing.

dose_modifications.condition: Capecitabine NCI-CTC Grade 3 toxicity - first occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt until resolved to grade 0-1 then continue at 75% with prophylaxis if appropriate

dose_modifications.condition: Capecitabine NCI-CTC Grade 3 toxicity - second occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt until resolved to grade 0-1 then resume at 50%

dose_modifications.condition: Capecitabine NCI-CTC Grade 3 toxicity - third occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Discontinue capecitabine

dose_modifications.condition: Capecitabine NCI-CTC Grade 4 toxicity
dose_modifications.notes: Discontinue unless consultant considers in patient's best interest to continue at 50% once resolved to grade 0-1

pre_medications: Metoclopramide 10mg oral three times a day when required (take home antiemetic)
Loperamide 4mg oral after first loose stool then 2-4mg four times a day when required (max 16mg/24 hours)
special_warnings: DPD testing required: All patients must be tested for DPD deficiency before initiation (cycle 1). Patients with complete or partial DPD deficiency are at increased risk of severe and fatal toxicity.
Indications: metastatic/advanced colorectal cancer OR adjuvant therapy of stage II or III colon cancer following surgery. 8 cycles; metastatic patients assessed for response after 4 cycles.
If chest pain occurs consider stopping capecitabine.
Total daily dose of capecitabine must be divided into two doses 12 hours apart. First dose administered in evening of day 1. Serious toxicity has occurred where total daily dose given twice a day.
Capecitabine should be taken with or after food.
This is a short course of oral chemotherapy that must not be continued beyond the prescribed schedule.
NPSA/2008/RRR001 must be followed when prescribing, dispensing or administering oral chemotherapy.
Neutrophil must be ≥1.5x10⁹/L and platelets ≥100x10⁹/L prior to prescribing.

drugs.notes: Intravenous infusion in 250ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 60 minutes. Dose banded per national dose bands (6mg/ml). Vesicant - extravasation precautions required. Must be administered via non-PVC administration set containing in-line 0.22 micron filter. Paclitaxel should always be administered PRIOR to carboplatin. Hypersensitivity reactions tend to occur with first or second infusion.

Intravenous infusion in 500ml glucose 5% over 60 minutes. AUC 2 using Calvert formula. Maximum dose 300mg. Dose banded per national dose bands (10mg/ml). Irritant - extravasation precautions required. Administered after paclitaxel.

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophils ≥1x10⁹/L
dose_modifications.notes: 100% of dose

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophils < 1x10⁹/L
dose_modifications.notes: Delay chemotherapy until neutrophils ≥1x10⁹/L then continue at 75% of original dose for all subsequent doses. Continue radiotherapy during delay.

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophils < 1x10⁹/L
dose_modifications.notes: Delay chemotherapy until neutrophils ≥1x10⁹/L then continue at 75% of original dose for all subsequent doses. Continue radiotherapy during delay.

dose_modifications.condition: Platelets 25-74x10⁹/L
dose_modifications.notes: Delay chemotherapy until platelets ≥75x10⁹/L then continue at 75% of original dose for all subsequent doses. Continue radiotherapy during delay.

dose_modifications.condition: Platelets 25-74x10⁹/L
dose_modifications.notes: Delay chemotherapy until platelets ≥75x10⁹/L then continue at 75% of original dose for all subsequent doses. Continue radiotherapy during delay.

dose_modifications.condition: Platelets < 25x10⁹/L
dose_modifications.notes: Delay chemotherapy until platelets ≥75x10⁹/L then continue at 75% of original dose for all subsequent doses. Continue radiotherapy during delay.

dose_modifications.condition: Platelets < 25x10⁹/L
dose_modifications.notes: Delay chemotherapy until platelets ≥75x10⁹/L then continue at 75% of original dose for all subsequent doses. Continue radiotherapy during delay.

dose_modifications.condition: Renal impairment CrCl less than 20 ml/min
dose_modifications.notes: Omit carboplatin. Note: significant changes in GFR of >10% may require dose adjustment.

dose_modifications.condition: Renal impairment
dose_modifications.notes: No dose adjustment needed

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment bilirubin > 1.25xULN and AST/ALT > 10xULN
dose_modifications.notes: Consider dose adjustment

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment bilirubin > 51 μmol/L
dose_modifications.notes: Paclitaxel not recommended

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment
dose_modifications.notes: No dose adjustment needed

dose_modifications.condition: Non-haematological NCI-CTC Grade 2 toxicity - first occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt chemotherapy until resolved to grade 1 or below then resume at original dose. Continue radiotherapy regardless.

dose_modifications.condition: Non-haematological NCI-CTC Grade 2 toxicity - second occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Resume at 75% of original dose. Continue radiotherapy regardless.

dose_modifications.condition: Non-haematological NCI-CTC Grade 2 toxicity - third occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Resume at 50% of original dose. Continue radiotherapy regardless.

dose_modifications.condition: Non-haematological NCI-CTC Grade 2 toxicity - fourth occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Discontinue treatment

dose_modifications.condition: Non-haematological NCI-CTC Grade 3 toxicity - first occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt until resolved to grade 1 or below; resume at 75% of original dose. Continue radiotherapy regardless.

dose_modifications.condition: Non-haematological NCI-CTC Grade 3 toxicity - second occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Resume at 50% of original dose. Continue radiotherapy regardless.

dose_modifications.condition: Non-haematological NCI-CTC Grade 3 toxicity - third occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Discontinue treatment

dose_modifications.condition: Non-haematological NCI-CTC Grade 4 toxicity - first occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Discontinue treatment

pre_medications: Chlorphenamine 10mg intravenous (30 minutes before chemotherapy, hypersensitivity prophylaxis)
Dexamethasone 8mg intravenous (30 minutes before chemotherapy, hypersensitivity prophylaxis - per regimen summary; 10mg per main text)
H2 antagonist per local formulary (30 minutes before chemotherapy): famotidine 20mg oral, OR nizatidine 150mg oral, OR ranitidine 150mg oral, OR ranitidine 50mg intravenous
Ondansetron 8mg oral or intravenous (antiemetic, 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy)
Dexamethasone 4mg oral once a day for 2 days (take home, starting day after chemotherapy)
Metoclopramide 10mg oral three times a day as necessary (take home)
special_warnings: Concurrent radiotherapy: 35-day cycle starting on the first or second day of radiotherapy. Chemotherapy given weekly on days 1, 8, 15, 22, 29 concurrently with radiotherapy.
Continue radiotherapy regardless of any delays to chemotherapy treatment.
Indication: neoadjuvant treatment of resectable cancer of the oesophagus or oesophagogastric junction.
Paclitaxel must always be administered PRIOR to carboplatin.
Paclitaxel must be administered via a non-PVC administration set with in-line 0.22 micron filter.
Paclitaxel is a vesicant - extravasation precautions required.
Carboplatin is an irritant - extravasation precautions required.

Hypersensitivity reactions tend to occur with first or second infusion of paclitaxel. Interrupt for minor symptoms (flushing, localised rashes); if resolved within 5 minutes, may restart at lower rate with intensive monitoring. Immediately discontinue for severe reactions (profound hypotension, bronchospasm, generalised erythema).
Maximum carboplatin dose is 300mg when using calculated creatinine clearance at AUC2. Seek consultant advice for obese patients or calculated CrCl >125ml/min.
EDTA or calculated creatinine clearance required before each treatment.
Significant changes in GFR of >10% may require carboplatin dose adjustment.
FBC, LFTs and U&Es required prior to each day of treatment.
Dose modifications apply to BOTH carboplatin and paclitaxel.
